name: Houthis (Yemen)
summary: Day 78-79 quiet — UKMTO recent-incidents page and Lloyd's List confirm no incidents recorded in the Red Sea since September 2025. Houthis have continued to make supportive statements about Iran but avoided resuming hostilities. **UN Security Council adopted Resolution 2812 (2026) extending Secretary-General's monthly reporting on Houthi attacks for six additional months until July 15, 2026 — formally acknowledging that the threat level has diminished** (CONFIRMED — UN Press Release, Wikipedia Red Sea crisis, gCaptain, UKMTO recent-incidents). Coordination-decay framework remains intact through Day 78-79. Houthi non-response now spans (a) Iran's May 4 multi-axis kinetic restart, (b) US Project Freedom + pause cycle, (c) the May 7 IRGC destroyer attacks, (d) the May 8 second day of US tanker strikes, AND (e) the May 8 Hezbollah Mt. Meron drone reach. The full proxy-restraint pattern is now multi-event confirmed: even when other Iranian proxies (Hezbollah) demonstrate first cross-border reach, Houthis remain quiet. Maritime Executive's 'Houthis Announce End of Red Sea attacks' framing remains uncorroborated by state action but is now structurally consistent. Persistent VLCC/Frontline rate elevation is HORMUZ-driven, NOT Bab el-Mandeb-driven (Frontline +13.5% MoM, BOAT +18.6% 3M).

active_triggers.condition: Houthis begin confirmed Red Sea attacks on NEUTRAL commercial shipping (verified by maritime trackers) — Day 47 attacks RESUMED but ONLY on belligerent-linked vessels (US/UK/Israeli). Neutral shipping
Houthis launch confirmed attack on Israel (missile/drone reaching Israeli territory)

recent_signals.headline: DAY 73-74 RED SEA QUIET — HOUTHI NON-RESPONSE TO IRAN MAY 4 MULTI-AXIS KINETIC RESTART INCREASINGLY ANOMALOUS; COORDINATION-DECAY THESIS STRENGTHENS
DAY 65 RED SEA QUIET — IRAN'S HORMUZ-FIRST PROPOSAL VIA PAKISTAN + IRGC 'MORE SEVERE DAMAGES' RHETORIC + LEBANON KINETIC
DAY 64 RED SEA QUIET — UKMTO UPDATE 034 EXPLICITLY DOCUMENTS HOUTHI RHETORIC-WITHOUT-OPERATIONAL-INDICATORS

historical_summaries.summary: 10 signals. Abdul Malik al-Houthi declared group 'prepared to enter the war' with 'fingers on the trigger' in recorded address | Mohammed al-Bukhaiti (political bureau member, not military commander) told NYT: 'the expansion of the conflict to include other countries, | Three weeks into war, no Houthi attacks

22 signals. Day 33: STILL zero confirmed Houthi attacks on Red Sea commercial shipping | CONFIRMED ABSENCE: Day 34 of war — ZERO confirmed attacks on merchant shipping in Red Sea or Bab el-Mandeb | Day 35: ZERO confirmed Red Sea shipping attacks
